reviews.review_msg: Becky was a very kind and caring professor! She explained the material very well and expressed a genuine desire for each student to do well. She provided us with many practice problems to work on every week to practice what we learned in lecture which helped immensely in exams. I would definitely recommend taking this course with Becky.

Genuinely wants you to succeed. This class was a retake for me to better my grade so I was extremely nervous to take it again, but she made it so easy and enjoyable to learn stats. Always very understanding and sweet, just talk to her! Would recommend to take her, our final average wasn't the best so she adjusted our letter grades.

Becky is one of the best profs I've had! I came into the course expecting to struggle, but Becky taught clearly, gave us lots of practice problems, and gave reasonable exams. She has a strong accent and speaks very quickly, but once I got used to her lectures I had no issues at all! She clearly wants her students to succeed. Highly recommended!

Becky is an amazing professor. You can tell she is really rooting for her students success! I usually hate math type classes and had put this class off for 3 years after starting my degree. I finally took it this semester as I need it for a requirement. I ended up loving statistics thanks to Becky, which I am absolutely shocked about.

Great prof! Her accent can be a bit hard to understand sometimes in lectures, but it's not as bad as people make it to be. Just do all the practice questions and tutorial questions and you'll be set for the midterm and final. Genuinely cares for the students in her class unlike a lot of other professors!

BEST PROF at sfu for me. she genuinely wants you to learn and is very approachable. Just attend her lectures and go through the practice qs and lecture slides. she makes the course easy and interesting. HIGHLY RECOMMEND taking a class with her.

The midterm and final were meant to be easy and were actually very hard (especially the final). The midterm was from chapter 1-15, the final was cumulative. I would recommend doing your own learning for this class as the lectures are content heavy and fast. Stay on track with weekly lectures and do all the practice questions that she posts.
her exams were very easy if you understood the basic concepts, do all her practice and you will be good, she doesn't test in depth but know CLT for midterm.
amazing prof, she's very kind and nice. Other than that you have to attend lectures and study in order get a good grade but its fair.

such a fair class she even gave bonus marks as a halloween treat. she really wants u to suceed. just make sure to attend class and know how to do everything she teaches. the midterms and finals are based on the practice MT and extra practice problems

Dr. Becky Lin is one of the most caring instructors I have met in MATH/STAT classes. She wants us to understand the concepts well through assignments, quizzes, and exams. These include endless practice problems and giving assignments in advance. She can also delay assignment due dates if a legitimate reason is given earlier.

She was the best prof I have ever had so far. Watch all the modules properly and you will be fine. If you want the best mark (A+), simply do the extra practice questions she posts for each unit and the midterm and final will be LITERALLY similar to those. She also gives so many assignments and online quizzes to help you boost your mark!!!
Makes the difficult content easy to understand with her explanations. Literally tells us what will be on the tests, and test/assignment/quiz questions are identical to practice problems.

Professor Lin is a good stats prof, like i suck at math and i passed. My one advise would be to study the modules!! Do not leave them for the last minute cuz it will be to difficult to cover all of them a week before your exams and go to her office hours for help. Professor Lin does a lot to help you, she genuinely wants you to succeed.

Becky is a supportive professor who genuinely wants her students to succeed. The course is challenging, but she provides lots of practice problems and sample exams that closely reflect the material. The only drawback was that the final topics felt rushed.

She is a really good prof, gives extra credit. For me, this class was a good GPA booster. If you are taking it online, pace yourself and don't fall behind on the lectures videos. Midterm was fairly easy (average was 73) and final was also fair game if you study (78 average). 
Clear grading criteria
Becky is amazing! Everyone should try and get her as a prof for any stats class she teaches! She is the best!

Becky was very clear with her examinations and was very accommodating. She genuinely cares about her students and wishes the to do well. Do all her practice and if you need help she is there and responds fast to emails. Take stats with her and you'll be set

Take this course! I'm not good at math or stats and I found this very straightforward. Becky does everything she can to support students, offering extra bonus marks and even telling us specifically what will be on exams. If you attend lectures she says "know this for the exam". Overall very great prof and would definitely take another course

Becky is an excellent teacher and really cares about her students. Her lectures are to the point and they always have extra information so its worth attending. Her exams are just like her practice so be sure to do those. The exams are very fairly graded and often curved. Overall a super enjoyable course as long as you do the work provided

She's amazing. I'm not good at statistics and I did well in her class. She has a way of explaining really hard concepts in a way that could make a toddler understand. She also allows written cheat-sheets on midterm day and final day. Bless you, Becky

Taking stats online, and the first half before the midterm is easy enough to understand with her accent. However, after the midterm the content begins to get much harder and half the stuff she says in her lecture videos is literally WRONG and is writing something completely different than what she's saying which often becomes REAAALLY confusing.

Her accent is very thick, which is not a problem except i rlly need to understand what she's saying and I cant. Im not the best at math so taking this with her is very hard because i dont even know what she's talking about. She does provide practice questions tho
love her. take her class. lots of practice questions and just such a sweet prof
great lecture slides and a lot of practice questions that help preparing for exams.

Becky is genuinely sweet and such a chill prof, literally my saving grace this semester - and it's my first one. Although the accent is a bit hard to understand, which is coming from someone of the same culture, there are recordings of the lectures going through the slides, and they're just definitely easy enough to understand!
Going to lectures is a waste of time. She has been changing her grading system for the past semesters. Do the practice problems for each chapter.
Many opportunities to get extra credit and uses questions straight from the practice exams (like 30%). You can write anything on the cheat sheet too. She's nice.

I'm gonna be fr- I did NOT deserve my B- I should've failed the course because I was so dumb and couldn't understand the course material (not math smart at all) but Becky always encouraged me and helped me as much as she could by explaining the concepts to me, and even giving out extra credit to people who simply attended lecture. TAKE HER COURSE!!

This class helped me get over my fear of quantitative courses, Professor Lin made everything so easy to understand. She broke down the logic behind the math so you have a better understanding why things happen/work the way they do, rather than just memorizing formulas or instructions. Her lectures were admittedly hard to get through, but worth it
I know there are comments about her accent, but it's still understandable. She scaled the finals by a lot too. She genuinely wants her students to get good grades, will take a course with her again.

Professor Lin always has the way of making complicated concepts easy to understand. Her classes are well-structured and clearly organized. The exam material is always reasonable, and as long as you carefully complete her practice questions, achieving a high grade is attainable. Overall, she is an excellent and responsible professor.

Becky's teaching is an organizational mess. Inconsistent, unexplained notation and concepts are weirdly generalized, if at all.
 
I think she tries to simplify harder content, which is great, but doesn't do so effectively. The content is easier compared to most, but it could be so much better if she focused on WHY things happen, not just results.

Dr. Lin is one of the best profs at SFU. Statistics is not an easy subject, but her slides and explanations make it digestible and easy to follow. If you do all the practice problems and homework and review the lectures, you are golden for the midterm and final. I attended a few of her online office hours and she is so caring, and super helpful!

If you need need to take stat203 as a required course, please take it with Becky! I suck at math and was scared to take this course after dropping it with swartz (terrible prof), but Becky breaks everything down and the TAs, especially Roxanne are so helpful. Be ready to study hard and consistently go to TA office hours with follow up Qs though.

Amazing professor, she clearly cares about her students a lot. She gives out lots of practice questions and makes sure you understand all of it. The exams were very fair, as some of the questions had the same format but worded differently. Would definitely take her classes again!

She clearly cares about her students and wants them to succeed. Her content reflects on her assignments, exams and quizzes, and everything is marked fairly. It's evident she puts in the work to help her students and although the content can be tricky, she is very good at explaining everything. The course itself is tricky but she makes it easier.

Dr. Lin is really caring and understanding towards her students as I once missed a quiz and she let me take it the next day. Her practice questions are very similar to exam questions so they're very fair. She gives opportunities for bonus marks and she posts lecture videos. She also responds to emails fast, usually within 10 min. Best prof ever.

i enjoyed becky's class so much. she was nice caring and even funny. she gave many opportunities for bonus marks. the exams were pretty much exactly like the practice materials. if i need to take another stats class i hope i can take it with becky.

Very caring and awesome professor. Her passion for her work is apparent and she genuinely wants all her students to do their very best. Granted, I was in a tough position when I took this class and ended up with a low grade. This WAS NOT the professors fault! She gives a lot of recourses and cheat sheets for midterms and the final! Very nice :)

Becky is the actual GOAT! She gives a lot of practice problems and are very similar to questions on the exams. Her accent is a little thick but her lecture slides are very self-explanatory. She wants to see her students succeed :)

The best prof I've had in uni so far. Accent is hard to understand, but she provides you with pre-recorded lecture videos and extra practice, which she sometimes uses as questions in the tests - this is enough to do good on the exams. Gives extra credit opportunities for in-person lectures. Allows aid sheets during exams. Very helpful and caring.

Becky is so sweet and a great prof! She made her lectures available online which was very helpful. Her tests were very fair and the practice problem sets she provided were very reflective of the exams. Don't hesitate to ask her questions during the exams as well she was very helpful at clarifying any confusions I had.

Becky is an amazing professor, you can tell she really cares about her students. Though she does have an accent making it difficult to understand at times, but the concepts are easy enough where it is not a massive issue. Make sure to do her practice problems and you will excel! (plus her daughter is also adorable!)

Very professional and helpful, but my low rating is from how the lectures are organized/taught. There's a lot of misuse/inconsistency in notation, and information is taught in a un-abstract/generalized way. Concepts were purposely unconnected (etc. never providing general forms for Std Errors or Conf Intervals), which doesn't help future courses.

Becky is a great professor. Most of her exam questions come from the chapter practice questions. She gives many opportunities for bonus marks. However she does have quite a heavy accent so it may be hard to understand sometimes but she really cares about you

Do her practice problems, all of them, it will ensure a A and you will understand the concepts. She really wants you to succeed. She is very accessible by emails. Collect the bonus marks. Amazing prof.

One of the best profs I've had at SFU. She really cares and wants you to do well. Most midterm questions are straight from her practice sets, so if you review the lecture notes and do all the practice questions, you'll be fine. She also grades pretty easily on assignments and tests

Most caring prof out there. Truly wants students to succeed! She provides so many opportunities for help, gives lots of practice problems, previous midterms, a cheat sheet, etc.That being said, the content itself was hard (I'm not a math head). Took a while to grasp-- she has a heavy accent making her difficult to understand sometimes.

I don't usually bother to write reviews, but I had to for Becky since she's genuinely one of the best profs I've ever had. She cares so much for her students and that is reflected in how much effort she puts into teaching. She has clear expectations for exams and choosing in-person vs online helped tremendously. Thanks for everything, Becky!

As a newcomer to stats, Dr. Lin made the material as easy to understand as possible, with multiple opportunities for bonus marks based on participation. Quizzes online were open book, assignments were uncomplicated, and support for exams was plentiful. Super well organized! I would recommend for anyone needing a stats requirement!!
Becky is the best prof you will ever have. she is very caring and actually wants you to do well.

This is the best prof I've ever had. Becky is so sweet and cares about her class and students so much. She puts so much effort into everything and wants everyone to do well. Would 100% recomend taking stats with becky.

Becky is AMAZING. she is so nice, caring and accessible outside of class. Her grading and assignments are all clear and definitely graded fairly. You do need to put effort in but if you watch the lectures, read her announcements, and do the practice problems you will do great. The class is super resourceful, and you can do everything online too.

Dr Lin is a great professor. She allowed us to take a bonus quiz and earn an extra 1% just by opening a crowdmark account. Her accent may be tough to follow but her exams are easy. Also, this class has no homework, but 3 quizzes, 2 midterms and a final. Overall great class, would take again with Dr Lin.

Retook this course after failing 203 with Swartz and I feel sane again!! Becky really cares about her students succeeding in her course and explains concepts clearly. She makes a lot of errors in practice/exam questions which was a little confusing to figure out, but she's the sweetest person ever so it's okay
She is the best of all time for Stat 201. You should definitely take it with her !
Her STAT100 is very easy. 10/10 would recommend for B-Sci.
LOVE HER. becky cares for her students and makes the class super easy to follow and provides recorded lectures. she is also very easy going on marking and exams are exactly like practice material.
BEST PROF IVE EVER HAD AT SFU
She has a heavy accent so I found myself relying a lot on online resources to learn. Her exams were very reasonable though and she is very nice and approachable.
An amazing stats professor, you won't regret taking her.

Becky sincerely cares about her students' success. I'm not a math person but had to take the course. She taught from the ground up (re-taught what we should already know from highschool). She would dedicate a lot of her free time to re-explaining concepts or answering student questions because she doesn't want us to feel any stress over school.
Becky is such a queeen! She's super insightful, helpful, and cares about her students.

If you are scared of math but you have to take STAT203, take it with Becky. The content was pretty straightforward for the first half of the semester. However, it gradually got more difficult. Becky gives you all the resources to succeed and is always quick to help. She does have an accent, so 2x lecture videos were not an option.

I loved being part of her class. She is really nice and caring for her students. She wants them to succeed.  Although she does have a small accent, you can still understand her. First half of the course was like grade 9 math. Second part of the course was like calculus.  However, if you do the practice problems  study a lot you'll do fine.

Exams are identical to the assignments, she did give a 2% bonus assignment.  
Provided extra practice problems as well, she does have an accent but if u read the lecture slides and textbook you will be fine and do well.

Her accent is very strong and at times it took me a while to understand what she was saying, but past that she is a good professor. Sometimes, I found that she overexplained topics, but then again you could just speed up the video. There is a textbook, but I didn't read it once and I did fine. Do the extra practice problems!

While the notes can be dense, Dr. Lin is amazing at responding to questions and concerns outside of her office hours or through email. Her quizzes take material directly from the extra practice problems so make sure to do them. Overall pretty good prof!
Very strong accent. Super confusing and unclear. Expects everyone to already know the course material. Course heavy in the sense that she posts multiple lectures for one week.

Becky provided lots of practice problems and was very clear about what would be on the exams. Her exams were achievable enough to get high marks. She's very sweet too! However, during our MT2, she made SEVERAL TYPOS on the exam and didn't even give us time/marks back to compensate for this. Could have screwed up a bunch of students.

Very kind and caring professor. Answers emails very quickly and tries to help you if you have any questions. Class is difficult but exams are based on the homework and assignments she assigns to help students do better. She may be a little hard to understand but she is still a good prof.

Becky is a really great prof. She gives bonus questions to boost our grades and is always super clear about marking and what's on the tests. She also allows cheatsheets. Sometimes her explanations can be a bit tricky to pick up in lectures. I only watched the lecture videos, didn't touch the textbook or practice questions much and did very well.

Although tests r worth a lot. They very a carbon copy of the assignments. Super easy. I never used the textbook once tbh. Really nice and easily accessible. She does have a strong accent but nothing too bad. Very caring. Easy TAs esp online course, they dont care.
she is a very sweet prof and wants her students to do well. gives a lot of bonus marks for just showing up to lecture. midterm and final were very fair.

Attendance isn't mandatory however, she does pop quizzes that counts as bonus marks and also highlights what's going to be on exams so it is recommended to show up for lectures. Assignments have guides that explains exactly how answers should be like. She clearly cares for her students and listens to advice.
very very very strong accent.

Becky is a great prof. Admittedly the accent is a bit hard but after a few classes you get used to it. Attendance is not mandatory BUT there are pop quizzes so show up (free 3% boost). She is very caring and wants to see you succeed. As long as you do the homework and make sure to know what each formula means you will do amazing in the class.
GOAT

Great professor. Even when I would email her during weekends shed respond ASAP. Content is hard but if you put the right amount of effort youll be fine. Only thing I would wish to be better are the solutions to the practice questions, sometimes hard to understand the way she writes the solutions. Wouldve loved clearer explanations

becky is the actual goat. her lectures are organized + easy to follow and she works step-by-step through examples, her assignments are very clear (she specifically states how to get full marks) and exams are exactly like the class examples, assignments and practice problems. she throws no curveballs and clearly wants to set everyone up for success!

Class was so hard but Becky is such a good prof!!!Her accent makes it difficult sometimes but she simply reads off the lecture slides and explains with examples,not hard to understand.She posts practice qs & tests, and assignments that help with future exams. Advice: put practice qs and lecture examples on cheat sheet. Good cheat sheet=pass

Took it in 2023 Summer term. Exams are similar to Exercises assigned, and detailed solutions are provided. As long as you complete and understand her Exercises, you will get good grades for exams. Her quick respond to emails helps a lot with questions I came up with.

Literally the most awesome perfect prof. Her assignments and practice midterms genuinely help so much and are so similar to her exams. Assignments are easy and the clearest grading criteria too. She actually is a blessing

Probably my best math prof till date, on par with Jamie. Literally such an angel. super understanding with midterms and cares a lot about the students if you show interest in the course. Very well organized and amazing to work with. I'd love to take another course with her

Provides lots of opportunities to gain bonus marks (but will not round due to so). Lectures are clear and directly related to assignments and exams. Overall she ensures that all students no matter there prior experience can do basic stats without causing too much confusion. Would STRONGLY recommend taking a course with her.

I improved my grade from a C- to an A- by re-taking this course with Becky. In order to do well in STATS you have to put in the work. Becky gives you ample opportunity to get bonus marks. Pre-read her lecture slides and learn all powerpoint material. Know her assignments as the questions appear for midterm and final / quizzes. She is GREAT.
Very strong accent, her point is never clear and leaves students puzzled, easy grader

Dr Lin a great prof overall, although her lectures may be confusing because of her accent she is willing to stay after lectures to fully explain concepts until you understand it. Her office hours are super accessible and she replies to emails with clear responses. The exams are made up of the homework questions and the practice exams she gives.

Her lectures were paced well, but may have been quick for some. She made up for the speed with how helpful she was after lecture/during OH. Her exams were very fair and easy if you did all the assigned problems.

For 270. I feel like her good rating on rmp is due to her likeable personality. She is not exactly what I would call a good lecturer. She zooms through material very quickly with no time for people to ask questions, and always spent too much time at the start of each lecture reviewing the previous lecture when we already did that in our own time.
This is for 270. Becky is a good professor, but she goes through the lecture material way too fast because she spends too much time on review. Her practice questions and answer key have many errors.

Class was ok. Prof allows you to bring formula sheets for exams which helps a lot. Exams are fair and prof prepares you well for them. Quizzes are easy if you do assignments. The annoying part about this class were the assignments because they involved coding (basic coding, but still annoying). DO PRACTICE EXAMS, it will 100% prepare you for test

Becky is a good prof who cares about your success. However, her strong accent makes it really hard to understand lectures and her doodles are hard to follow on the projector. The good part is, her practice midterms and finals are very helpful and oftentimes practically the same on the actual exam. Plus, you get cheat sheets. Overall good prof.

She is very clear on what you need to know for the midterm and final (gives a study guide for each). And she pretty much gives you the answers to the homework assignments. Class average was a B, don't know how people could fail her class.

I enjoyed taking this class with becky. Even though I skipped several lectures, I kept up with the slides and did well on the exams. Her exams were based on the slides and homework assignments so they were fair. She wants you to succeed. If you try in this class, you can easily get an A.

If you're ready to put in the effort, this class could easily be an A+. She makes the course easy for you to succeed. Exams are like practice Qs/midterms/assignments. First week, you get used to her accent and if you show up/put in the effort, you'll understand her with no problem. Her grading is 100% transparent. TAKE HER CLASS IF YOU CAN
Professor Becky is great. She really cares about her students, and it is easy to get a good grade if you put in the effort. Highly recommend!

Ms. Lin is someone very caring, she really wants her students to succeed in her course and if you put the work in, you will be just fine. Her lectures are very heavy tho, she talks really fast and her accent makes it even harder to understand. Do all the assignments, quizzes and DO THE PRACTICE MIDTERM + FINAL EXAM (super helpful)

Take a class with this prof given the chance - one of the best profs. Teaches in a clear manner, quizzes, midterm, and final were really fair. If you put in the effort, you can do well with this prof.

She is an amazing professor who wants you to succeed. If you don't understand something in her office hours she goes over everything. She has extra credit bonus Rstudio courses which are great practice and 1% extra. Highly recommend Dr.Lin!

Becky is the most caring and helpful prof I have ever had. She repeatedly asks you to come to her for help if you get stuck. She walks you through the solution. Even if you get stuck with coding problems she will personally find the error and fix it and send you fixed code. She *really* wants students to succeed. 6/10 for lecture clarity though.

Dr. Lin really wants you to succeed and it shows. She lets you bring cheat sheets to exams and is very clear on her criteria and expectation. Her lectures are difficult to understand at times, but she has everything you need to know on the slides which makes studying easy. I would definitely recommend if you're willing to work a bit.

Homework & practice exams prepare you well for exams but her lecture slides are loaded with text and its very hard to understand her sometimes, combined it makes lectures unbearable to sit through. If you ask her to explain a concept she is very helpful + exams/assignments are fair, not a bad professor but lectures are not good/worth your time.

I have always been terrible at math, and I was very nervous about taking statistics! Becky was extremely thorough with her lessons and made the material easy to understand. She was always willing to help when I was struggling. It ended up being my favourite course of the sem, and I recommend her to anyone needing to take a Q course!

FOR STAT 201: At first I really struggled with the course but Dr. Lin provided me with extensive help and motivated me. She helped me understand the course content when I did not understand. She was very fair and provided extra opportunities to improve our marks. I highly reccomend Dr. Lin, she is knowledgeable and very kind.

AN INCREDIBLE TEACHER! The class was less memorization and more application which is what most classes should be doing. Her teaching ability is very great but her english is very awful ngl. The exams were VERY FAIR and her quizzes were easy. She emails fast. She has practice problems too which helped me the most within this course.

tons of assignments which eases the stress of needing to do well on the exams. semi-difficult material but she truly does want her students to succeed. a very fair professor and the quizzes are an easy way to make up for marks you may have missed in the homework or exams.

Becky warrants a rating because of just how incredible she is as a prof. I took Stats 201 with her and not only did she care about every one of her students and their success, but she makes stats genuinely fun to learn. The work is heavy and the assignments are frequent, but I actually came out as a solid stats user and hope to further pursue stats

Took STATS 201 with Becky and she explained concepts well and made it easy to understand with diagrams/pictures and detailed explanations. She provides practice problems for every chapter which is helpful. Also very accessible for help. She really cares about her student's success. Expect to work for your A, but definitely recommend her!

Her lectures are absolutely attrocious. She really needs to practice her english before teaching. I am a native english speaker and even I have a very hard time discerning what she's trying to say about 25% of the time and im really not kidding its that bad. If you're an ESL student I cant even imagine how much pain youll be in for her course

Good prof for stats. Clear explanations and very accessible for help. Gives practice problems for every single chapter, especially helpful for quizzes and exams. Have to put in the work for good result, but overall is great professor and definitely recommend.

If your planning on taking STATS 201/203, take it with Becky! This was my first ever STATS course and I found that Becky made it really easy to understand. She provides a lot of practice which is helpful. The final exam instruction was a bit unclear this FALL 2021 semester, but overall great professor!

For someone who does not have a math background, Becky made Stats easy to understand. I found Becky to be super responsive, passionate, and dedicated to her student's success. Yes, there is a lot of course content, quizzes, and assignments. But this was in benefit to students success. This is definitely a class you should expect to work for your A

Contrary to what other students say, I definitely recommend Becky. Her lectures were very helpful and I believe students who didn't watch struggled. She provides practice problems, which really helped with exams. Also, she teaches how to code using R, so make sure you practice using the codes provided in lectures.

STATS is a really challenging course especially for someone not coming from a math background but Becky was such a great prof. Without a doubt the best stats prof I've met at SFU. She is so caring and you can see she genuinely wants her students to love stats and succeed. The final exam is challenging but she is always accessible for more help.

Pros: Fair Grader -Genuinely tries to improve and seeks feedback Very good at practical/applied teaching. Want to learn about data analytics? Take her course! Good notes Cons: Hard final, but was curved so it was fine. Took a few classes to realize that the students didn't have a good fundamental math background. However, she adjusted.

While Becky cared about her students, her explanations were more confusing than just learning from the textbook. We were told that using R Jupyter (a programming language for stats) would be optional but it ended up being required for some assignments and we were never properly taught how to use it. Grades on the final averaged around 50%.

Becky made us use a program which required a minor amount of coding which I did not understand or enjoy at all. The finals were also very unorganized with us getting important info the regarding the final less then 24 hours before we took the test, ON TOP of that the average for the final was below 50%. Avoid if possible

She certainly understands statistics very well, but can sometimes be hard to understand so I had to supplement by watching videos from other profs. I found she had poor wording for some of the questions on the final which caused confusion & stress. There were last minute changes to the exam that caused undue stress. Avoid taking her if possible.

Becky is one of the best statistics instructors. Too bad she's only sessional here. She knows what she's doing and is always upfront with students. A truly dedicated teacher and wants her students to do well and enjoy learning about statistics. It's just a hard subject so people turn to hate their prof but really, she's amazing.

Great prof. Lots of people don't like her cuz she has an accent & sometimes it's hard to understand what she's saying. But she's really passionate about statistics, and she takes the time to explain until you understand, when you have questions. She's also very accessible by email & replies quickly. Very fair and kind & looks out for her students.

Dont take this class. for the love of God do not put yourself through this absolute hell. her lectures are super hard to understand, barely any time to do the exams so you have to rush, and shes just horrible and not considerate. the class avg was too high so she made the final proctored on zoom. literally the night before she made the choice.

Do not take this class. Lectures don't cover nearly anything covered by the exams and assignments. Assignments are heavily time-consuming and you learn more by watching online videos of other profs teaching the course. Very hard exams and barely any time.

exams are extremely difficult and the assignments are very time consuming. Her office hours are helpful but if you ask questions and try to understand she gets mad. Any announcements she makes or email she sends, she complains or makes excuses about the other things she has to do. Very unprofessional and rude, avoid this prof.
